title: Odoo Technical Training and Interview Preparation Guide
content:
Bilkul tension mat lo! Tumhare college mein Target Integration aa rahi hai aur maze ki baat ye hai ki tumhare sources mein se ek video khud isi company ka webinar hai. Ye tumhare liye bahut bada advantage hai kyunki tumhe pata chal jayega ki wo log kya sochte hain aur unki values kya hain.
Maine tumhare liye ek Odoo Developer Learning Path banaya hai jo beginner level se interview ready hone tak ka flow follow karega. Isko hum total 6 Parts mein divide karenge.
Abhi hum Part 1 se start karte hain:


Part 1: Company Profile aur Odoo ka Introduction
Is part mein hum dekhenge ki Target Integration kya hai aur Odoo as a software kya kaam karta hai.
1. Target Integration ke baare mein (Source 1 & 2):
  • Target Integration ek specialized consultancy hai jo businesses ko digital transform karne mein help karti hai.
  • Inke paas 14+ years ka experience hai aur ye Odoo ke Gold Partner hain.
  • Pro-Tip: Interview mein agar tumne mention kiya ki tumne unka webinar dekha hai jisme Gordon Cooney aur Kostas Parquelos ne Odoo implementation ke baare mein samjhaya tha, toh interviewer bahut impressed hoga.
2. Odoo kya hai? (Source 4, 54, 55):
  • Odoo ek All-in-one Business Management Software hai jise ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) kehte hain.
  • Iska matlab hai ki ek hi software se company apne Sales, Accounting, Inventory, HR, aur Manufacturing sab manage kar sakti hai.
  • Ye Open Source hai, yani iska code available hai aur companies ise apni zaroorat ke hisaab se customize kar sakti hain.
3. Odoo ki implementation strategy (Kostas's Advice): Target Integration ke expert Kostas ne 5 important baatein batayi hain jo tumhe backend logic likhte waqt dhyan rakhni hain:
  • Keep it Simple: System ko zarurat se zyada customize mat karo, modularity ka use karo.
  • Modular Approach: Odoo "App-based" hai, yani pehle wahi apps install karo jo zaroori hain.
  • Standard vs Custom: Hamesha koshish karo ki Odoo ki "Out of the box" functionality use ho, coding tabhi karo jab functional configuration se kaam na chale.
  • Version Evolution: Odoo har saal ek naya version nikaalta hai, isliye code aisa likho jo future upgrades mein break na ho.

Part 2: Technical Architecture & Environment Setup
Ab jab tum samajh gaye ho ki Odoo kya hai, toh technical side par chalte hain.
1. MVC Architecture (Source 72): Odoo MVC (Model-View-Controller) pattern follow karta hai:
  • Model: Data kaise store hoga (Python classes and PostgreSQL database).
  • View: User ko screen par kya dikhega (XML files).
  • Controller: Jab user kisi button par click karega toh kya logic chalega (Python logic)
model view architecture woh architecture hota h jisme ki jo hum file nhi bante ek file data base seconeciton dekhgegi jo model hogyi ek file routing krenge jo controller hogi or ek file jo user pe data degi 

2. Python aur Odoo (Source 37, 65): Odoo ka core Python par bana hai. Interviewer tumse Python ke basic aur advance concepts puch sakta hai kyunki saara backend logic Python mein hi likha jata hai.
3. Odoo Versions aur Hosting (Source 20, 26, 37):
  • Abhi market mein Odoo 16 aur 17 kaafi popular hain.
  • Hosting Types: Odoo ko 3 tarah se host kiya ja sakta hai: Odoo Online (SaaS), Odoo.sh (Cloud), aur On-Premise (Apna server).

Part 3: Custom Module Development aur Mandatory Fields ka Logic
Odoo mein jab bhi humein koi naya feature chahiye hota hai, toh hum "Standard Code" ko chhedte nahi hain, balki apna ek Custom Module banate hain.
1. Custom Module ka Structure (Source 68):
Ek basic custom module banane ke liye aapko ye steps follow karne hote hain:
  • Addons Folder: Sabse pehle Odoo ke addons folder mein apne module ke naam ka ek naya folder banao.
  • __manifest__.py: Ye aapke module ki Identity Card hai. Isme module ka naam, version, author, aur dependencies (yani ye module kin doosre modules par nirbhar hai) likhi jaati hain.
  • models.py (Logic): Yahan aap Python code likhte ho jo database mein table banata hai.
  • Views (XML): Yahan aap decide karte ho ki user ko screen par fields kaise dikhengi.
2. Field ko "Mandatory" (Zaroori) kaise banayein? (Source 32, 33, 34)
Kai baar requirement hoti hai ki user kisi field ko khali na chhode (jaise Student ka naam). Iske Odoo mein do tarike hain:
A. Python ke zariye (Backend Way): Agar aap chahte ho ki database level par hi restriction lag jaye, toh model file mein field definition ke saath required=True likh do.
  • Example: first_name = fields.Char(string="First Name", required=True)
  • Intuition: Iska fayda ye hai ki chahe user UI se data bhare ya kisi API se, system use khali field save nahi karne dega.
B. XML ke zariye (Frontend/UI Way): Agar aap sirf screen par user ko rokna chahte ho, toh XML file mein field tag ke andar required="1" add kar do.
  • Example: <field name="last_name" required="1"/>
  • Intuition: Ye tab kaam aata hai jab aap kisi existing field ko (jo standard Odoo mein optional thi) sirf apne view mein mandatory banana chahte ho.
part 4: Automation aur Reordering Rules (Source 49)
Business ko chalane ke liye automation bahut zaroori hai. Target Integration ke ek tutorial mein Reordering Rules ke baare mein bataya gaya hai.
  • Kya hai ye? Socho ek dukandaar ke paas 100 biscuits hain. Wo chahta hai ki jaise hi stock 20 se kam ho, system apne aap supplier ko order bhej de.
  • Setup kaise karein?
    1. Product form par jao aur use "Stored Product" mark karo (kyunki track karna hai).
    2. Ek Vendor aur Cost set karo (taki system ko pata ho kis se kharidna hai).
    3. Minimum Quantity (Trigger): Wo point jahan order trigger hoga (jaise 20 units).
    4. Maximum Quantity: Wo target quantity jo humein maintain karni hai (jaise 120 units).
  • Result: Jaise hi stock niche jayega, Odoo apne aap ek RFQ (Request for Quotation) bana dega. Purchasing team ka kaam kitna aasaan ho gay
Part 5: Advanced Customization - Website Restrictions (Source 104-108)
Target Integration jaise projects mein aksar website features ko restrict karne ki demand aati hai.
  • Scenario: Aap nahi chahte ki jo log logged-in nahi hain (Public Users), wo product ki Price dekh sakein ya Add to Cart kar sakein.
  • Logic:
    • Isme hum Controllers ko extend karte hain.
    • Hum ek check lagate hain request.env.user._is_public(). Agar user public hai, toh hum use price hide kar dete hain ya 404 error page dikha dete hain.
  • Intuition: Ye advanced level coding hai jahan hum model aur view ke upar jaakar HTTP Requests ko control karte hain.

Part 6: The Final Showdown - Interview & Coding Round

  • Odoo Community vs Enterprise (Source 7, 18, 19):
    • Intuition: Community "Free" hai par limited features ke saath. Enterprise "Paid" hai par isme zyada features, mobile app, aur Odoo ka official support milta hai.
    • Key Point: Enterprise mein data migration free hota hai, jabki community mein khud karna padta hai.

  • Hosting Types (Source 20, 21):
    • SaaS (Odoo Online): Bilkul free server, par isme custom code nahi dal sakte.
    • Odoo.sh: Cloud platform jahan custom modules dal sakte hain. Ye developers ke liye best hai.
    • On-Premise: Apne khud ke server par install karna.

  • ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) (Source 66, 67, 72):
    • Intuition: Odoo mein humein database se baat karne ke liye lambe SQL queries likhne ki zaroorat nahi padti. Hum Python code likhte hain aur Odoo ka ORM use apne aap SQL mein badal deta hai. Isse galtiyan kam hoti hain.

2. Coding Round Solutions (Source 39 - 47)
Odoo ke coding test mein aksar 4 tarah ke sawal aate hain. Inka logic samajh lo:
  • Array Challenge (Arithmetic vs Geometric) (Source 40, 41):
    • Sawal: Ek numbers ki list di jayegi, pehchanna hai ki wo Arithmetic progression (AP) hai ya Geometric (GP).
    • Logic: Sabse pehle consecutive numbers ka difference check karo (2nd - 1st, 3rd - 2nd). Agar sab barabar hain, toh wo Arithmetic hai. Phir unka ratio check karo (2nd / 1st). Agar ratio same hai, toh wo Geometric hai.
  • String Challenge (Capital M & N Rules) (Source 42, 43):
    • Sawal: String mein 'M' aaye toh pichle character ko duplicate karo, 'N' aaye toh agle character ko uda do.
    • Logic: String par loop chalao. Jab 'M' mile, toh result string mein result[-1] ko firse append kar do. Jab 'N' mile, toh ek flag set kar do taki agla character skip ho jaye.
  • SQL Challenge (Source 44, 45):
    • Isme aksar Reports aur Average Age nikalne ko kaha jata hai. GROUP BY aur AVG() functions ki practice kar lena.
  • Graph Challenge (Shortest Path) (Source 45, 46):
    • Ye thoda tough hota hai. Isme aapko do points ke beech ka sabse chhota rasta nikalna hota hai. Iske liye BFS (Breadth-First Search) algorithm ka intuition hona zaroori hai.
